title: Sweep stale Fabric artifact names across portfolio docs (3 renames)
description: Complete the cross-surface rename sweep that was started but never finished for three renamed Fabric artifacts. The spec was corrected each time; the surrounding docs, commands, and support documents still present the OLD names as active. Align all non-spec surfaces to on-disk reality, with per-file judgment to preserve legitimate historical/convention/activity-name references. Audit-derived from coherence audit 2026-06-21 finding C-01; resolves FR-001 and completes FB-001's incomplete sweep.
acceptanceCriteria: All active/current references to `semantic_model_oeminsightbi` replaced with `OEMInsightBI_v2` across the non-spec doc/command surfaces; the old name remains ONLY in fabric/archive/ and the designated historical/tracking surfaces (spec archived-notes, feedback/archive.md, prior audit dirs, friction.jsonl, dashboard FR row).

All active/current references to `clean_columnsAndHeaders.Notebook` (the artifact) replaced with `bronze-to-silver.Notebook`; the pipeline ACTIVITY name `clean_columnsAndHeaders` in error_handling_strategy.md and the naming-convention DISCUSSIONS (naming_standards.md, fabric-artifacts-inventory.md 'Consider renaming') are preserved unchanged.
All active/current references to `report.Report` replaced with `report2.Report`; historical/archived references preserved.

README.md directory tree (lines ~33-36) shows the three on-disk artifact names (bronze-to-silver.Notebook, OEMInsightBI_v2.SemanticModel, report2.Report); docs/architecture/fabric-artifacts-inventory.md no longer tags any non-existent artifact as '✅ Active'.

A verification grep confirms the three old names resolve only under fabric/archive/ and the explicitly-designated historical surfaces — no active-context occurrences remain (positive control: the three new names appear as the live artifacts).
No new broken paths introduced — spot-check that every replaced path exists on disk; FR-001 marked resolved on completion.
notes: AUDIT-DERIVED: created from coherence audit 2026-06-21 finding C-01 (.claude/support/audits/coherence-2026-06-21-2141/findings.md#C-01). NOT a spec-decomposed task — the spec is already correct; this aligns the surrounding docs/commands to on-disk reality (hence the empty drift-fingerprint fields).

ROOT CAUSE: three Fabric artifacts were renamed on disk and the spec was corrected each time, but the cross-surface doc sweep was never completed. (1) semantic_model_oeminsightbi -> OEMInsightBI_v2 [FR-001, open friction]; (2) clean_columnsAndHeaders.Notebook -> bronze-to-silver.Notebook [FB-001, archived as 'resolved' but only the spec's 4 refs were fixed]; (3) report.Report -> report2.Report. Raw pre-judgment footprints: 46 / 40 / 15 occurrences respectively.
DISK TRUTH (verify before editing): fabric/OEMInsightBI_v2.SemanticModel/, fabric/bronze-to-silver.Notebook/, fabric/report2.Report/ are canonical; the old names exist ONLY under fabric/archive/.

PER-FILE JUDGMENT REQUIRED — do NOT blind sed-replace. Preserve: (a) historical/archived notes (spec 'old name archived in fabric/archive/' lines, fabric/archive/* contents, .claude/support/feedback/archive.md, prior audit dirs under .claude/support/audits/, friction.jsonl FR-001, dashboard.md FR-001 status row); (b) naming-convention DISCUSSIONS (standards/naming_standards.md, fabric-artifacts-inventory.md:64 'Consider renaming...'); (c) the pipeline ACTIVITY name 'clean_columnsAndHeaders' in error_handling_strategy.md (the orchestration activity, distinct from the notebook artifact — e.g. activity_name= usages).

DECIDE DELIBERATELY (do not silently rewrite task history): stale task-file files_affected/legacy arrays (task-002/003/004/009/013/014/015/016, 006, 012, 013) point at old artifact paths as historical provenance of completed work. Recommend LEAVING them unless re-pathing is explicitly wanted — they record what was true when the task ran. These task files are intentionally NOT in files_affected; surface this choice in the completion report.

PRIORITY ORDER within the sweep: start with README.md (portfolio front door — dir tree lines 33-36 show all three stale names at once) and docs/architecture/fabric-artifacts-inventory.md (falsely tags clean_columnsAndHeaders L20 and semantic_model_oeminsightbi L33 as 'Active').

DOES NOT BLOCK CORE WORK: pipeline scheduling (task-010) and performance (task-012_*) take the critical path. This is portfolio-presentation hygiene. Parallel-safe with 010/012_* (zero files_affected overlap — verified at audit time). cross_phase:true so it is not gated behind the pipeline phases.

WHY P2 (not P3 like the DQ polish in task-020): this is the portfolio front door — a recruiter cross-checking README against fabric/ hits an immediate mismatch, and the artifacts inventory makes false 'Active' claims. Higher visibility than internal DQ-check polish, still not on the build critical path.

ON COMPLETION (orchestrator): set FR-001 status -> resolved in .claude/support/friction.jsonl. Consider adding a lightweight regression guard (a grep assertion that the three old names appear only under fabric/archive/ + the designated historical surfaces) so a future doc edit can't silently reintroduce the drift — this is the recurrence the audit caught.

CLAUDE: autonomous — all edits are local markdown/JSON aligning docs to on-disk reality; no Fabric UI action needed (the renames already happened in Fabric; disk reflects them). Surface the task-file-provenance decision (above) in the completion report for Erik's confirmation.
ERIK: review the diff (especially README.md + fabric-artifacts-inventory.md) and confirm the task-provenance-array choice.

Split into 5 subtasks BY FILE-GROUP (021_1a root/portfolio, 021_1b docs/architecture+guides, 021_1c .claude commands, 021_1d support/documents) + 021_2 verification gate. Chose file-group over by-rename because the three renames OVERLAP on shared files (README.md, fabric-artifacts-inventory.md, project_definition.md all carry all three old names) — a by-rename split would force those files to be edited by multiple subtasks and break parallel-safety. 1a-1d have disjoint files_affected (fully parallel-safe); 021_2 depends on all four and makes the global 'old names only in archive' assertion no single group can. The per-file judgment rules (preserve activity name / naming-convention examples / historical notes) are distributed into the subtask that owns the affected file — 021_1d carries the heaviest judgment load.

--- Parent auto-completed: all 5 subtasks (1a/1b/1c/1d + 021_2 gate) Finished with passing per-task verification. Cross-surface Fabric artifact-rename sweep complete across 22 files (3 renames). Global assertion confirms no active doc/command surface retains a stale name; FR-001 resolved; regression guard tests/test_doc_artifact_names.py passing (6/6). Open follow-ups surfaced during the work: FR-002 (_Measures.tmdl has no v2 equivalent) and FR-003 (error_handling/incremental_load use a stale 'clean_columnsAndHeaders' activity identifier; real activity is 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning').
